questions.question: List the three main versions of SNMP and state one key characteristic of each. (4 marks)
keyPoints: SNMPv1: The original version, uses community string-based security (plaintext).
SNMPv2c: Introduced new PDUs like GETBULK for efficiency, but retained the insecure community string model.
SNMPv3: Added crucial security features like authentication (USM) and encryption for secure communications.

questions.question: Explain the SNMP manager-agent architecture. Describe with examples how SNMP can be used for monitoring and controlling a simple IoT device, such as a smart light bulb. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Definition of Manager (NMS) as the control center and Agent as the software on the managed device.
Role of the MIB in defining manageable parameters (e.g., light status OID, brightness level OID).
Use of GET Request operation to monitor current state (e.g., is the light on? What is its brightness?).
Use of SET Request operation for control (e.g., turning the light on/off, setting brightness to 50%).
Use of TRAP/Notification for event-based monitoring (e.g., agent sends a message to manager if the bulb burns out).
The importance of using SNMPv3 in a home/public network for security.

questions.question: Compare the core SNMP PDUs (GET, GETNEXT, GETBULK, SET, TRAP, INFORM) and explain the purpose of each. (4 marks)
keyPoints: GET retrieves a specific OID value; GETNEXT traverses the MIB tree sequentially for table walking.
GETBULK (v2c+) optimizes bulk data retrieval by filling responses with multiple variables in one request.
SET modifies a variable on the agent for remote configuration and control.
TRAP is an unsolicited async alert from agent to manager; INFORM is like TRAP but requires acknowledgment for reliability.

questions.question: Compare SNMP and NETCONF-YANG as IoT management protocols. Discuss scenarios where each protocol is more appropriate. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Transport: SNMP uses UDP (connectionless, lightweight) vs NETCONF uses SSH/TLS (connection-oriented, secure).
Data modeling: SNMP uses MIB with SMI (flat, limited) vs YANG provides rich hierarchical models with constraints and validation.
Operations: SNMP offers simple GET/SET vs NETCONF provides full CRUD with transactional support (commit, rollback).
SNMP is preferred for resource-constrained IoT devices needing basic monitoring and alarm generation due to low overhead.
NETCONF-YANG is preferred for complex device provisioning, configuration orchestration, and gateway management requiring structured models.
In practice, both may coexist: SNMP for lightweight sensor monitoring, NETCONF-YANG for gateway and controller management.
